tools.text: Goblin Tools：一套专为神经多样性人群设计的轻量 AI 工具集，其中 Magic ToDo 能把一个笼统的任务自动拆解成可执行的微步骤。适用场景：克服任务启动困难和「不知道从哪下手」的瘫痪感。
Saner.AI：面向 ADHD 的 AI 个人助理，整合笔记、邮件、日程，用自然语言管理所有碎片信息。适用场景：把散落各处的想法、待办和提醒集中到一个 AI 大脑里。
Motion：AI 日历和任务管理工具，能根据优先级和截止日期自动排布你的一天，任务延误时自动重新规划。适用场景：解决 ADHD 的时间盲和过度承诺，让 AI 替你做日程决策。
Reclaim.ai：AI 日程防御工具，自动为习惯、任务和休息时间在日历上预留并保护时间块。适用场景：防止日程被会议填满，为深度工作和恢复留出空间。
Tiimo：视觉化的日程与计划 App，专为神经多样性设计，用图标、颜色和倒计时让时间「看得见」。适用场景：对抗时间盲，把抽象的时间转化为视觉信号。
Brain.fm：基于神经科学的 AI 功能性音乐平台，用特定节奏的声音诱导专注、放松或睡眠状态。适用场景：用声音环境帮助 ADHD 大脑更快进入并维持专注状态。
Focusmate：虚拟共同工作（body doubling）平台，把你和另一个真人配对进行计时专注 session。适用场景：利用「身体在场效应」对抗拖延和孤独工作时的分心。
Structured：可视化的每日时间线规划 App，把一天排成清晰的视觉时间轴。适用场景：让 ADHD 用户对一天的节奏有直观掌控感。
Lex：内置 AI 的写作工具，能在你卡壳时续写、生成大纲、克服空白页恐惧。适用场景：解决 ADHD 写作启动困难和组织思路的难题。
Mem：AI 驱动的笔记工具，自动整理和关联你的笔记，无需手动建立文件夹结构。适用场景：适配 ADHD 不擅长手动归类整理的特点，让 AI 自动建立知识连接。
ChatGPT：通用对话式 AI，可充当 ADHD 的「认知协作者」：拆解任务、起草文本、整理思绪、模拟教练。适用场景：作为随叫随到的执行功能外挂，弥补工作记忆和组织能力的不足。
Claude：Anthropic 的对话式 AI，以长文理解和更自然的对话著称，适合深度梳理复杂任务。适用场景：处理长文档、深度对话式思考整理。
Otter.ai：AI 会议转录工具，自动记录、转写并总结会议要点和行动项。适用场景：弥补 ADHD 在会议中走神、记不住要点的问题。
Reflect：AI 笔记工具，强调网状链接和 AI 辅助回顾。适用场景：帮助 ADHD 把碎片想法连成网络。
Routinery：习惯例程 App，把晨间/晚间例程拆成带计时的步骤序列。适用场景：用结构化例程对抗 ADHD 的日常混乱。
Todoist：任务管理 App，带自然语言输入和 AI 辅助功能。适用场景：快速捕捉待办，减少遗忘。
Forest：游戏化专注 App，专注时种一棵虚拟树，离开则树枯死。适用场景：用游戏化机制和损失厌恶对抗手机分心。
RescueTime：自动时间追踪工具，记录你在各应用和网站上花的时间并生成报告。适用场景：帮 ADHD 看清「时间都去哪了」。
Perplexity：AI 搜索引擎，直接给出带引用来源的答案而非一堆链接。适用场景：满足 ADHD 的好奇心，让探索式学习更高效不易跑偏。

findings.text: Recent advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) are opening new frontiers in the diagnosis and treatment of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD).
findings.source_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in ADHD Diagnosis and Treatment: A New Frontier in Neurotechnology | IntechOpen

findings.text: AI-powered technologies such as machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics have the potential to enhance ADHD assessment by analyzing complex patterns in large datasets, such as neuroimaging, genetic information, and behavioral data.
findings.source_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in ADHD Diagnosis and Treatment: A New Frontier in Neurotechnology | IntechOpen

findings.text: These technologies can support clinicians in early diagnosis and personalized treatment planning.
findings.source_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in ADHD Diagnosis and Treatment: A New Frontier in Neurotechnology | IntechOpen

findings.text: The diagnosis and treatment of ADHD involve significant challenges due to symptom overlap with other neurodevelopmental conditions, particularly Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD), leading to diagnostic uncertainty, especially in cases of comorbidity [11, 12].
findings.source_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in ADHD Diagnosis and Treatment: A New Frontier in Neurotechnology | IntechOpen

findings.text: These challenges are further amplified during times of crisis, such as the COVID-19 pandemic, when external stressors and disruptions in routine—particularly within educational settings—can intensify symptom presentation and complicate differential diagnosis [13, 14].
findings.source_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in ADHD Diagnosis and Treatment: A New Frontier in Neurotechnology | IntechOpen

findings.text: Variations in diagnostic criteria across regions further contribute to inconsistencies, resulting in both over- and underdiagnosis in different jurisdictions [15].
findings.source_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in ADHD Diagnosis and Treatment: A New Frontier in Neurotechnology | IntechOpen

findings.text: Accurate diagnosis is especially difficult in populations with intellectual disabilities, where symptoms may present atypically, and subjective clinical judgments can lead to misdiagnosis unless supported by standardized tools [16].
findings.source_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in ADHD Diagnosis and Treatment: A New Frontier in Neurotechnology | IntechOpen

findings.text: In adults, overlapping symptoms with conditions such as depression can obscure underlying ADHD, complicating diagnosis [17].
findings.source_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in ADHD Diagnosis and Treatment: A New Frontier in Neurotechnology | IntechOpen

findings.text: Treatment outcomes depend heavily on accurate diagnosis and recognition of comorbidities, requiring a combined pharmacological and psychosocial approach [18, 19].
findings.source_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in ADHD Diagnosis and Treatment: A New Frontier in Neurotechnology | IntechOpen

findings.text: Just as the diagnosis and treatment of complex psychiatric conditions like ADHD involve multifaceted challenges, another major transformation is unfolding in modern medicine: the rise of artificial intelligence (AI).
findings.source_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in ADHD Diagnosis and Treatment: A New Frontier in Neurotechnology | IntechOpen

findings.text: While AI holds tremendous potential to enhance the accuracy, efficiency, and accessibility of healthcare, its integration into clinical practice requires careful ethical and regulatory consideration.

findings.text: Through technologies such as deep learning, natural language processing, and machine learning, AI is being applied in various domains—including medical imaging and predictive analytics—with diagnostic performance often comparable to human experts [21, 22, 23].
findings.source_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in ADHD Diagnosis and Treatment: A New Frontier in Neurotechnology | IntechOpen

findings.text: However, concerns remain regarding the limited clinical validation of many AI tools, issues of algorithmic transparency, and patient data privacy [24, 25].

findings.text: Recently, a buzz rippled through the mental health community when a study published in partnership with Seoul National University Bundang Hospital claimed a massive breakthrough.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: Researchers developed an Artificial Intelligence (AI) model that could screen children for Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) using nothing more than a simple retinal photograph.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: The study, which prospectively recruited children from Severance Hospital and Eunpyeong St.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: Mary’s Hospital, produced results that were staggering: the AI reportedly achieved an accuracy rate of 96.9%!
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: While headlines promised a revolution in mental health screening, a deeper look into this research and the study’s design has exposed that this 96.9% AUROC was more likely evidence of a flawed methodology rather than a biological reality.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: To build their screening tool, researchers analyzed over 1,100 retinal images using a digital pipeline called AutoMorph and a machine-learning model known as XGBoost.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: Because dopamine dysregulation influences how blood vessels grow and remodel, the study hypothesized that an ADHD brain would leave a unique "fingerprint" on the retinal vasculature, resulting in denser, thicker vessel structures.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: On paper, the logic was sound: use AI to spot the subtle vascular remodeling caused by dopaminergic shifts.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: Instead, this study created a time-traveling dataset:
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: A scientific study is only as reliable as its control group.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: The control in any experiment acts as a baseline against which the study group is compared.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: In this study, the control group wasn't composed of healthy children from the community.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: When training AI, you must never allow the "test questions" to leak into the "study material." The researchers, however, committed a fundamental violation of machine learning hygiene known as Eye-to-Eye Data Leakage.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: The study split the data by the eye rather than by the participant.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: If a child's left eye was used for training and their right eye was used for testing, the AI was effectively "cheating." Instead of learning the general traits of ADHD, the model was potentially memorizing individuals.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: This error artificially balloons accuracy metrics.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: The true test of medical AI is diagnostic specificity, or differential diagnosis.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: While the model claimed 96.9% accuracy against a flawed control group, its performance collapsed when faced with real-world complexity.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: When the researchers asked the AI to differentiate between ADHD and Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD), the accuracy plummeted to a poor 63% AUROC.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

findings.text: In real-world clinical settings, an accuracy of 63% is dangerously close to a 50% coin flip.
findings.source_title: The Retina as a Mirror: Decoding the ADHD AI "Breakthrough" and Its ...

concepts.executive function: 执行功能（计划、组织、启动、工作记忆等大脑管理能力）
concepts.working memory: 工作记忆（短期保持和操作信息的能力）
concepts.time blindness: 时间盲（难以感知时间流逝和估算时长）
concepts.body doubling: 身体在场效应（有人陪伴时更容易专注完成任务）
concepts.dopamine: 多巴胺（与动机和奖励相关的神经递质，ADHD 大脑相对缺乏）
concepts.task initiation: 任务启动（开始一项任务的能力，ADHD 常见困难）
concepts.rejection sensitive dysphoria: 拒绝敏感性焦虑（RSD，对批评和拒绝的强烈情绪反应）
concepts.hyperfocus: 超聚焦（ADHD 在感兴趣领域的高强度专注状态）
concepts.cognitive load: 认知负荷（大脑同时处理信息的负担）
concepts.neuroplasticity: 神经可塑性（大脑通过训练改变结构的能力）
concepts.emotional dysregulation: 情绪失调（难以调节情绪强度和恢复）
